compatibility_report:
SONIC X SHADOW GENERATIONS (2513280)

Issue transferred from https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/8984.
@gbendou posted on 2025-08-19T18:13:15:

# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: SONIC X SHADOW GENERATIONS
- Steam AppID of the game: 2513280

## System Information
- GPU: GTX 980 Ti
- Video driver version: nvidia 570.169
- Kernel version: 6.8.0-78-generic
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/gbendou/c40a0a6ba29824c196eca0ff84778824
- Proton version: 10.0-2

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? --> 
Game worked perfectly on Proton 10.0-1. Once updated to 10.0-2, I began to experience incessant and significant slowdowns and jittering. Hotfix was marginally better, Experimental was about the same. I tried 9.0-4 and the game won’t launch no matter what version I use ever since. Rebooting my computer, verifying the files, deleting the prefix folder and reinstalling the game didn’t help.


## Reproduction
Try switching Proton versions, I guess.

[steam-2513280.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21862117/steam-2513280.log)

# Compatibility Report

## System Information

- Proton version: 1727105898 experimental-9.0-20240918b (tested 8, 9 - same issue) 

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.


## Symptoms 
When launching the game it crashes shortly after the launcher loads with a winevulkan assertion failing (see below)
![steam-game-crash](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5a3b2145-eb4a-4c71-b9a4-7252d7dd94d6)

Edit: There are now reports on the Ara discord that AMD GPUs seem to work

## Reproduction
1. Buy expensive game claiming steamdeck support
2. Launch on PC
3. Observe crash shortly after launcher loads

Note: This does not reproduce when PROTON_LOG=1, the game just generates gigabytes of log within seconds and never gets to the banner crash, but let me know how many lines you want me to attach, if this is useful at all. 
[steam-2021880-short.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/17125111/steam-2021880-short.log)

[steam-1539620.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21821738/steam-1539620.log)

- Proton version: Experimental

## I confirm:
- [ Y] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [ Y] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->

Whenever the game requires any text to be input the game freezes and exits. For example at the start of the game you are asked your name it will show the box to add the name but as soon as you type anything it exits.


## Reproduction
Start new game progress to when it asks for your name, type anything.

## Note
Even when running this on windows the game behaves oddly it will have the screen go black for moment before you begin typing its almost like the game is changing displaymode e.g Fullscreen to Boarderless but I cant confirm this

- Proton version:10.0-1

Full Sys Info as Gist: https://gist.github.com/Dal1ster/8b6db57a8f8085cffa5e68863be0f01c
## I confirm:
- [ x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x ] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.



## Symptoms
Throughout the game, it tries to mess with the player by taking non steam screenshots and saving them in what would be the document folder. Under proton however, it fails to save anything in the games compatdata folder. The game also suffers from stutters and freezes, which can last from a few seconds to the point where you have to restart the game itself.

## Reproduction
Set proton version, play through the game until you see what looks to be a filepath in the top left of the screen and the steam screenshot sound to play. then check the compatdata folder for that filepath at /home/LINUXUSER/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/3658110/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/Documents/ and notice there is no screenshots or anything of the sort.

# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Microsoft Flight Simulator (A32NX extension). This is a **very popular** extension for MSFS. See https://flybywiresim.com/. I create a new compatibility report because it's unrelated to the main game which runs fine.
- Steam AppID of the game: 1250410

## System Information
- GPU: AMD ATI Radeon 5700 XT
- Driver/LLVM version: NAVI10, DRM 3.42.0, 5.15.6-gentoo, LLVM 13.0.0, 4.6 (Compatibility Profile) Mesa 21.3.1
- Kernel version: 5.15.6-gentoo
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/freijon/4191b914c6707d66141224d2226b943e):
- Proton version: experimental-6.3-20211209

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
When starting a flight (press the "Fly" button) the loading process starts and runs for a while. Suddenly the game crashes to Desktop. In the console I can see the following error:
`Unhandled exception: unimplemented function api-ms-win-crt-runtime-l1-1-0.dll.feclearexcept called in 64-bit code (0x000000007b0123be).`

[steam-1250410.log.tar.gz](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/7690312/steam-1250410.log.tar.gz)

## Reproduction
1. Install the A32NX extension for MSFS
2. Select the new Aircraft
3. Start the flight by clicking on the "Fly" button.

# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Creatures Docking Station
- Steam AppID of the game: 1659050

## System Information
- GPU: Intel HD Graphics 4000 (IVB GT2)
- Driver/LLVM version: Mesa 21.2.2
- Kernel version: 5.15.5-76051505-generic
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/truboxl/d89d8496d53cea55cb358acaa5c00ac2
- Proton version: Proton Experimental

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
Unable to launch the game. Getting the error:

"The game can not continue: Please change your display settings to be 'High Color (16 bit)'"

![Screenshot from 2021-12-20 22-23-09](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/12658589/146782160-a72cc44c-b8d9-4c68-9868-d935b429062f.png)

## Reproduction
Simply launch the game and choose "Play Creatures Docking Station"

## Notes
There simply no easy way to set 16bit color depth in Linux.

Wine suggest we have to fiddle with `/etc/X11/xorg.conf.d/` or `/etc/X11/xorg.conf` and set something along the lines of `DefaultDepth    16`. But that will cause Steam not to start. See https://wiki.winehq.org/FAQ

```
10.4 Graphics
10.4.1 My application complains about being unable to change the resolution or color depth
You generally need to edit the Screen section of your /etc/X11/xorg.conf to support additional color depths and resolutions. There may also be a problem with Xrandr.
```

Another method is to use Xephyr. See https://wiki.winehq.org/256_Color_Mode
So far adding this line to Launch Options works for me:

```
Xephyr :2 -ac -resizeable -screen 1280x720x16 & env DISPLAY=:2 %command%
```
Additionally need to add these registries to `steamapps/compatdata/1659050/pfx/user.reg` or use `protontricks`
to disable window decoration and window manager control
since there is no window manager running in Xephyr.
Running `mutter` or another WM in Xephyr seems to mess things up for me.
```
[Software\\Wine\\X11 Driver] 1639931810
#time=1d7f4f69fbce18e
"Decorated"="N"
"Managed"="N"
```
Going full screen and then back to windowed screen seems to break the game however. Audio still running though. `Alt+Enter` sometimes fixes the problem.

# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Shatterline
- Steam AppID of the game: 2087030

## System Information
- OS: GNU Guix GNU/Linux
```console
$ guix describe
Generation 4	Sep 22 2022 07:58:11	(current)
  nonguix 415aeda
    repository URL: https://gitlab.com/nonguix/nonguix
    branch: master
    commit: 415aeda016ca174764a700d8493cc93285ebe3dd
  guix 04cad8e
    repository URL: https://git.savannah.gnu.org/git/guix.git
    branch: master
    commit: 04cad8e29ed85be838921c7fa05f7c5bce94b6d1
```
- Arch: x86_64
- Model: Z68A-D3-B3
- CPU: i7-2680K
- GPU: AMD RX 570
- Driver/LLVM version: AMDGPU (Linux 5.19.8)
	- LLVM: 11.0.0
	- DRM: 3.47.0
	- VBIOS version 113V34122-F3
	- Arch: POLARIS10
	- Mesa: 21.3.8
-  Drivers in flatpak: Driver Version:  4.6 (Compatibility Profile) Mesa 22.1.7 (git-7a3414c4bd)
- Kernel version: GNU/Linux 5.19.8
- Window Manager: GNOME Shell
- X Server Release: 12101004
- 
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): No
- Proton version: Experimental and 7.0-4 tried

## I confirm:
- [NO] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [X] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->

After verifying game files this is all it gives me:

```console
$ cat ~/.var/app/com.valvesoftware.Steam/steam-2087030.log 
======================
Proton: 1660576899 proton-7.0-4
SteamGameId: 2087030
Command: ['/home/kreyren/.var/app/com.valvesoftware.Steam/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/common/Shatterline/Launch_Game.exe', '-platform_type', 'Steam', '-bugsplat_enabled']
Options: {'forcelgadd'}
depot: 0.20220919.70
pressure-vessel: 0.20220919.0
scripts: v0.20220823.0-0-gcc4e44f
soldier: 0.20220919.0 soldier 0.20220919.0
Kernel: Linux 5.19.8 #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC 1 x86_64
======================
```

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
The game install**ed** fine and ran great (platinum) with d3dcompiler_47 and win7 until 22.09.22 when it got updated and now i can't launch it after checking file integrity and before i couldn't join any match (likely anti-cheat)

1. Failure to load textures resulting in texture-less environment:

Fixed by adding `d3dcompiler_47` though winetricks

2. Unable to join any game:

Originally fixed by setting the protonprefix on Windows 7 until 22.09.22 where game got updated **and it's broken again**

3. On bordless fullscreen window sometimes after the game loads into the match it causes me to be unable to move excluding looking around with mouse (unable to shoot) and unable to change window so i have to always change in TTY and kill it in `htop`, hotfix to this was to play the game in windowed.

## Reproduction

1. Get Steam from Flatpak on any GNU distro with Linux kernel
2. Install Shatterline on steam
3. Try to play the game

## Additional notes
* Some people on protondb are complaining about shutter during gameplay which is not present on my setup
